data.content: The issue is clear. The target generates the file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so MSBuild doesn't know to compile it. The fix is to include the generated file in compilation:

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates the file before compilation (`BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`), but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but MSBuild doesn't know it should be compiled.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group using `<ItemGroup>` with `<Compile Include="..." />` inside the target, after generating the file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add generated file to compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

    <ItemGroup>

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This is the standard pattern for targets that generate source files before `CoreCompile` — you must dynamically add them to the `Compile` item group within the same target.
